title: 1 Samuel: David's Rise
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 1 samuel: david's rise and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 1 samuel: david's rise and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What reason did God give Samuel for choosing David over David's older brothers? Answer: God looks at the heart, not outward appearance.
segments.correct_answer: God looks at the heart, not outward appearance
acceptable_variants: the LORD looks on the heart not the outward appearance
man looks at the outside but god sees the heart
God does not judge by height or appearance — he sees the inner man

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The pouring of oil on a person's head to set them apart for a role; Samuel anointed David privately in 1 Samuel 16 and the Spirit came upon him, beginning his preparation for kingship
The Philistine champion whose challenge David accepted in 1 Samuel 17; David's victory with a sling and stone established his public reputation and demonstrated that the battle belongs to the LORD
